lines.text: That's Trace — and it is not a chatbot you query.
It's an agent that watches your team, and speaks up the second you contradict yourselves.
Observe. Remember. Reason. Detect. Guard. Learn.
This is the thread that settled it. Nobody scrolls back this far.
Nobody typed a query. Karthik reaches for Mongo — cited. Pushes on-prem — cited, and dated, and owned. Raj starts a queue Sam already shipped — duplicate, caught.
And when you do want to ask — it answers. Cited.
A human grades the catch, and it re-cognifies into memory. The agent gets sharper for this team.
And when there's nothing to say — it says nothing.
Underneath is one Cognee temporal knowledge graph.
When you drift, it doesn't search a database — it walks the graph back to the exact decision, and pulls it into the light.
One graph-completion call retrieves the prior decision and judges the contradiction, in a single hop.
A decision made in chat — enforced on code. No other memory tool closes that loop.
Here's the twist. In 2026, your code isn't written by one engineer who forgets. It's written by a whole fleet of them.
Watch — before it writes one line, the agent asks Trace: has the team already decided this? Postgres. Decided by Priya. Mongo, rejected. So it corrects itself.
One endpoint. Every agent. Same brain — so the whole fleet stops re-making the same rejected calls.
Cited. Live. Checkable.
And it watches the people, not just the code.
Ask what happens if she's out — and every consequence is cited to a real message. No invented numbers.
Trace. It observes, remembers, reasons — and it acts. Nobody has to ask.
Your team's memory — with a pulse.
